underlying: Republican margin of victory, 2026 Alabama U.S. Senate general election (Nov 3, 2026). OPEN seat: incumbent Tuberville running for governor.
method: Anchor: 2020 Tuberville defeated Doug Jones by ~20pts (62.5-37.5). Trump won AL by ~30pts in 2024. State PVI deep R+15. 2026 is an OPEN seat (Tuberville running for governor) — increases uncertainty modestly. Trump-endorsed Barry Moore in primary; Marshall and Hudson also competitive. Likely D nominee will not be high-profile. Modeled R margin ~ N(25, 8). Sigma elevated due to open seat and competitive primary that may produce a less polished nominee than incumbent.
notes: Open seat — Tuberville running for governor. Trump endorsed Moore. Generic ballot D+5 nationally is a modest headwind, but AL is R+15 PVI. Historical AL Senate margins: Shelby 2016 +28, Sessions 2014 uncontested, Jones 2017 special +1.7, Tuberville 2020 +20, Britt 2022 +37 (Britt got bigger margin than Tuberville). Mean of recent contested: ~22. Slightly higher mu vs Tuberville's 20 because open-seat R nominee likely faces weaker D and rides Britt-like environment. Kalshi market YES asks: P19 0.74, P21 0.67, P25 0.53, P29 0.36 — implies mid-quote median ~24-25, well-aligned with our 25.
